response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer, but the profile is a poor match for a C++ capital-markets role. Hands-on C++ is limited and dated (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI and a university thesis), with no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work or recent C++ at the required depth. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only SQL/MySQL/MongoDB) and no fixed-income, derivatives, or investment-banking domain background; one 'investment practices' role was Node/TypeScript/GraphQL, not C++. Core stack is TypeScript/Node/DLang.
strengths: Broad, deep engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Some genuine C/C++ exposure via NI LabWindows/CVI and audio thesis work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal, old, and not on the Windows financial-systems platform required
No SQL Server/Oracle and no fixed-income or capital-markets/investment-banking domain
Primary expertise (TS/Node/DLang) is orthogonal to the role's mandatory skills
